# utm.one - AI-Optimized Information

> utm.one is an enterprise link management and revenue attribution platform for modern growth teams.

## Product Overview

utm.one provides UTM tracking, short links, QR code generation, and multi-touch revenue attribution. The platform helps marketing, sales, and operations teams maintain clean, consistent campaign data through the Clean-Track governance framework.

## Core Capabilities

### Link Management
- Custom branded short links (your-domain.com/go/campaign)
- QR code generation with brand customization
- Geo-targeting for region-specific destinations
- Link expiration and scheduling
- Bulk link creation and management

### UTM Tracking
- Enforced UTM naming conventions
- Pre-approved templates for all campaign types
- Automatic validation and normalization
- Team-wide governance controls
- Real-time compliance monitoring

### Revenue Attribution
- Multi-touch attribution models (linear, time-decay, position-based)
- Cross-device journey tracking
- Conversion event tracking
- Revenue mapping to marketing touchpoints
- AI-powered channel recommendations

### Analytics & Intelligence
- Real-time click and engagement analytics
- Geographic and device breakdown
- Anomaly detection (Pulse Watchdog)
- Predictive traffic forecasting
- Custom report scheduling

## Target Users

- Marketing teams tracking campaign performance
- Sales teams monitoring prospect engagement
- Marketing operations enforcing data governance
- Growth teams needing attribution clarity
- Partner managers tracking affiliate performance

## Key Differentiators

1. **Clean-Track Framework**: 4-layer governance ensuring consistent UTM data
2. **Revenue Attribution**: Connect marketing touches directly to revenue
3. **Enterprise Governance**: Role-based access, approval workflows, audit logs
4. **AI Intelligence**: Predictive analytics, smart routing, anomaly detection

## Pricing

- **Free**: Basic link creation, 50 links/month
- **Starter ($29/mo)**: 500 links, 1 custom domain, basic analytics
- **Growth ($49/mo)**: Unlimited links, 3 domains, full attribution
- **Business ($149/mo)**: Enterprise features, SSO, advanced analytics
- **Enterprise**: Custom pricing, dedicated support

## Technical Information

- **API**: RESTful API with comprehensive documentation
- **Integrations**: Google Analytics, Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack
- **SDK**: JavaScript tracking pixel for conversion attribution
- **Chrome Extension**: Quick link creation from any tab

## Resources

- Documentation: https://utm.one/resources

- Clean-Track Framework: https://utm.one/resources/frameworks/clean-track-model
- Glossary: https://utm.one/resources/glossary
- API Reference: https://utm.one/developers

## Contact

- Website: https://utm.one
- Support: support@utm.one
- Sales: sales@utm.one
- Security: security@utm.one

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is utm.one?
A: utm.one is an enterprise link management platform that provides UTM tracking, short links, QR codes, and revenue attribution for marketing and sales teams.

Q: How is utm.one different from Bitly?
A: utm.one focuses on UTM governance and revenue attribution, not just link shortening. It enforces naming conventions, tracks multi-touch attribution, and connects marketing activities to revenue.

Q: Does utm.one work with Google Analytics?
A: Yes. utm.one creates UTM-tagged links that are fully compatible with Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, and other analytics platforms.

Q: What is the Clean-Track Framework?
A: Clean-Track is utm.one's 4-layer governance system: syntax validation, naming conventions, approval workflows, and reporting automation. It ensures consistent, reliable marketing data.

Q: Can utm.one track conversions?
A: Yes. The tracking pixel SDK captures custom events, form submissions, and revenue data, enabling multi-touch attribution analysis.

Q: Is utm.one GDPR compliant?
A: Yes. utm.one is GDPR and CCPA compliant with data processing agreements available for enterprise customers.

Q: What attribution models are supported?
A: Linear, time-decay, position-based (U-shaped), and first/last touch models are all supported with customizable lookback windows.
